seoTitle: How to Add Code Snippets and Blocks in Slack
metaDescription: Format code in Slack using inline monospace text, multi-line code blocks, and syntax-highlighted snippet files for large scripts.
intro: Dropping raw code into a Slack channel without formatting turns into a chaotic wall of text. This guide covers inline code formatting, multi-line code blocks, and using dedicated snippet files for longer scripts.

steps.heading: Format inline code
steps.body: Highlight a short code fragment and click the inline code icon, or wrap it manually with single backticks.

steps.heading: Open a code block
steps.body: Press Shift+Enter for a line break, then click the code block icon to open a standalone formatted region.

steps.heading: Type a multi-line block manually
steps.body: Alternatively, type three backticks on a fresh line to trigger a full code block container automatically.

steps.heading: Set syntax highlighting
steps.body: Use the formatting ribbon's code dropdown to select the correct language for accurate syntax highlighting.

steps.heading: Create a snippet for large scripts
steps.body: Click the plus icon in the message bar and choose to create a snippet file for logs or code too long for a normal message.

faq.question: What's the difference between inline code and a code block?
faq.answer: Inline code wraps a short fragment within a sentence, while a code block formats multiple lines as a standalone monospace region.

faq.question: Can teammates edit a shared snippet file?
faq.answer: Yes, depending on permissions, collaborators can edit or comment directly on a shared snippet.

faq.question: Does Slack support syntax highlighting for all languages?
faq.answer: Most common programming languages are supported, though some obscure or newer languages may not have dedicated highlighting.

faq.question: Can I download a snippet after it's posted?
faq.answer: Yes, snippet files can typically be downloaded directly from the message.
